world.title: The U.S. Lost the Iran War 'On the Strategic Level'
world.description: The B-52 Stratofortresses loaded with JASSM-ER cruise missiles were approaching the Middle East when Pakistan's diplomatic intervention succeeded and Trump accepted Iran's ceasefire proposal — the same 10-point plan he had rejected a week earlier. China played the decisive role, providing guarantees to Iran that the U.S. would honor any agreement, while simultaneously refusing to meet with Trump on trade until the war ended. The ceasefire terms strengthen Iran: a toll booth on the Strait of Hormuz collecting tens of billions annually in Chinese yuan, complete sanctions relief, and the regime intact. T

world.title: Ethiopian police arrest human trafficking ring responsible for 3,000 victims
world.description: The human trafficking network has been under investigation since 2018, police said in a statement. The ring recruited young people from Ethiopia, Sudan, Eritrea, Djibouti, Kenya, and Somalia who hoped to migrate to Europe through Libya.

ai.title: AI Deception: Man Arrested for Controversial Video after Plane Crash
ai.description: A man named Uddhav Bhagwan Kapse has been arrested for creating an AI-generated video related to the Baramati plane crash. The video, which defamed Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis, gained significant attention before being removed. Kapse was detained in Jalna by Mumbai's Cyber Cell.

ai.title: Drones and AI take flight to combat mosquito-borne disease
ai.description: As warming temperatures spread dengue to new regions, Stanford researchers are using AI-powered drones to hunt down hidden mosquito breeding sites. Anyone who has left water standing in a wading pool or empty flower pot knows how quickly mosquitoes arrive. But these backyard nuisances can spawn serious diseases: dengue, chikungunya, and Zika are all transmitted by mosquitoes breeding in water that collects in shallow containers.

ai.title: Adobe’s Student Spaces AI tool in Acrobat lets you create guides, mind maps and summaries
ai.description: Adobe’s new feature, Student Spaces, in Acrobat is a free platform that helps students manage their workload by consolidating notes, files, and study resources. It includes an AI assistant, interactive quizzes and flashcards, and options for different learning preferences. The platform also allows for group learning and collaboration on projects, aiming to enhance productivity.
